meta.title: Safe Haven MFB
meta.descriptor: Core banking dashboard for a microfinance bank

stats.label: Bank staff trained on new UI

blocks.text: Safe Haven Microfinance Bank serves small business owners and traders across four states in Nigeria. Their operations team was managing loan approvals, account management, and compliance reporting across **four separate legacy systems** — some of them spreadsheet-based.

I was brought in to design a unified core banking dashboard that would consolidate these workflows into a single, auditable interface — and do it in a way that bank staff with varying levels of digital literacy could adopt without months of retraining.

I spent two weeks embedded at a branch in Ikeja, shadowing loan officers, tellers, and the compliance team. The biggest insight was that the inefficiency wasn't in any single system — it was in the _handoffs_ between systems.

items: Loan applications were started in one system, verified in a second, and approved in a third — with email threads bridging the gaps.
Tellers had no visibility into a customer's loan status, leading to repeated customer explanations at the counter.
Compliance reports were assembled manually each month, taking 3 days and introducing transcription errors.

blocks.text: I designed around a unified customer record — every touchpoint in the bank anchored to a single view of the customer. Loan officers, tellers, and compliance staff each had a role-specific view, but they were all looking at the same underlying data.

blocks.alt: Safe Haven MFB unified dashboard showing customer record with loan status, account balance, and transaction history
blocks.caption: The unified customer record — visible to all roles, filtered by permission level.

blocks.text: Before this, I had to open three tabs and cross-reference before I could answer a customer question. Now it's one screen.
blocks.attribution: Senior Teller, Safe Haven MFB Ikeja branch

blocks.text: The dashboard shipped in Q4 2022 after a 3-month development sprint. Loan processing time dropped by 60% in the first quarter. The compliance team now generates their monthly reports in 4 hours rather than 3 days.
